title: Whose Fault Is It Anyway Shinji vs His Authorities
display_title: Whose Fault Is It Anyway Shinji vs His Authorities
wikitext:
[]: Whose Fault Is It Anyway? (Shinji vs His Authorities)             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 27, 2013 12:40 pm [Split from Has Evangelion Changed Or Is It The Audience? - Monk] Chuckman wrote: This is what confuses me, as I try to figure it out. Shinji should, apparently , be listening to his peers and superiors, but what they're telling him is often, if not usually, wrong! What the hell is he supposed to do?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Sep 27, 2013 3:23 pm Bagheera wrote: When did his peers and superiors ever steer him wrong

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 27, 2013 3:29 pm pwhodges wrote: If they steered him right, why did they then proceed to punish him for the results?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Sep 27, 2013 3:41 pm Well, we've established our respective views of both of those in more relevant threads, I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Sep 27, 2013 4:19 pm Bagheera wrote: They didn't. They punished him for the part he did all by his lonesome.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sat Sep 28, 2013 9:02 am I would think that the title of the thread is a false dichotomy. A true dichotomy contains a direct logical negation: Either Shinji was at fault, or Shinji was NOT at fault. Shinji not being at fault doesn't directly require that His Authorities be at fault. It could be that nobody was completely innocent nor completely at fault (which is most certainly the case). It's just the consequence of not having enough information and/or the ability/desire to communicate it effectively. Which is essentially one of the major themes in NGE and arguably moreso in Rebuild. Shinji and Misato were both "right" at the end of 2.22 in terms of the objective they wanted to reach. Shinji grew a pair and decided he needed to save Rei; Misato cheered him on because he's finally becoming someone who is willing to shoulder some responsibility and act on his own... Since all the information they have is that if an Angel reaches Lilith 3I would be started, neither of them knew that Shinji was awakening the Eva, or that awakening the Eva will trigger 3I. It was something they couldn't have foreseen. The only person to "blame" here would be Fuyutsuki, in my opinion. Gendo has an ulterior motive, that we know for sure. But Fuyutsuki has arguably less of an agenda and should be more neutral. But he is just going by what Gendo says and does.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Sep 28, 2013 10:36 am ElMariachi wrote: Problem is that Misato spent all of 1.0 and 2.0 pushing him into doing things for himself("It doesn't matter what other wants, what to you want?! I you want to leave, no one will stop you!" "Honestly, I don't think I really care for the sake of mankind either" "Go for it Shinji-kun! Don't do it for the others! But to accomplish your own wishes!"),

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Sep 28, 2013 3:03 pm Bagheera wrote: Misato didn't just want a mindless drone -- she wanted him to be fully committed to saving the world because it was important to him , not her. She was trying to motivate him to act for the right reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Sep 28, 2013 4:42 pm ElMariachi wrote: No, she wanted him to find a personal reason for piloting, any one, how could she want him to fully commit to saving the world when even her don't give a shit about saving the world, by her own admission!

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Talos » Sat Sep 28, 2013 4:48 pm ElMariachi wrote: No, she wanted him to find a personal reason for piloting, any one, how could she want him to fully commit to saving the world when even her don't give a shit about saving the world, by her own admission! She can't want him to act for the right reason because herself is in NERV for the wrong ones : vengeance. She even got called on it by Ritsuko with her insane plan to defeat Sahaquiel : Misato is so obsessed by her personal vendetta against the Angel that she utterly refuse to cede one inch to them, even if it mean charging heads-on one who's falling at super-sonic speed from orbit!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Sep 28, 2013 6:53 pm Bagheera wrote: But that's bullshit, since she's made a career out of doing just that. Just because she doesn't think much of humanity doesn't mean she wants the world to die, or that she doesn't care about her friends. You can say "fuck the world" and not really mean it (indeed, that's your primary defense when we point out that Shinji said just that when he went after Rei! No fair going hyper literal now when you're willing to play fast and loose when it suits you!).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Sep 28, 2013 7:04 pm ElMariachi wrote: It worked, but thanks to a pure miracle : absolutely everyone(even the pilots) were telling her that this plan had almost no chances to succeed and that she should take a safer approach, the fact it worked don't change the fact that plan had extremely high risks that could have been at least reduced, if only by ordering to transfer Lilith in case the Eva pilots failed to stop Sahaquiel.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Sep 28, 2013 7:30 pm Bagheera wrote: She didn't have the authority to do that. More importantly, I didn't see anyone offering alternatives. "Oh Major, your plan is insane!" "Yeah, got it. What else should we do?" "I dunno, but your plan is INSANE!" "Okay, go with that. Meantime, let's save the world."

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Sep 28, 2013 7:42 pm ElMariachi wrote: That's why I've added that's it if Misato is punishing Shinji for having acted upon his own wishes, unaware of what would happen, forgetting that she was the one who incited him to act like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sat Sep 28, 2013 8:20 pm There it is again! That uncanny feeling of deja vu! I recall reading and discussing this topic once before but don't remember when......Where have we heard all this before? Oh wait .

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Sep 28, 2013 8:47 pm Bagheera wrote: But she didn't. As I said, "Fight for your own reasons" is not the same as "do whatever the hell you want."

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Talos » Sat Sep 28, 2013 10:46 pm ElMariachi wrote: it seems from Shinji's POV that Misato is punishing him for having done exactly that, acting upon his own wishes, like he wished this fucked up situation to happen.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Sep 29, 2013 3:24 am Bagheera wrote: But she didn't. As I said, "Fight for your own reasons" is not the same as "do whatever the hell you want."

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Sep 29, 2013 4:07 am pwhodges wrote: That would be a problem only if he then did something that he should have known would be disastrous. Since there was no way that he could have known what was going to happen, or that it was even possible, criticising him for doing it is to my mind perverse.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Sep 29, 2013 5:43 am @ Dr. Talos : ht's why I said that it's important to remember that it's from Shinji's POV, from an outside POV Misato's now advices(or rather orders this time) makes perfect senses, but from Shinji's POV for whom barely hours before she was advising him to do almost the exact contrary it's really confusing. And as you said WILLE had too little time to clear everything before ReiQ attacked, so the fact he chose to escape with her was impossible to prevent. What I'm saying now is a big "if" : Shinji feels that Misato is punishing him for acting the way she incited him to, and if it appears in FINAL that it's actually the case, then that would make Misaot a big hypocrite, IF that's the case. On a side note, the chain of events during the interrogation scene is also why I think that Gendo must have a way to spy on the Wunder and was actually monitoring Shinji's briefing : Rei Q's timing was unnaturally just perfect so Shinji would chose to follow her, just after WILLE said the two things that antagonized him the most(the DSS Choker being a punishment and that Rei is "doesn't exist anymore") but just before they had the time to clear what they meant, it's too perfect of a timing to be a coincidence, especially when Gendo is concerned! Now I get the feeling that most of us is saying the same thing, that responsibility is at best no ones because there wasn't any way to predict what would happen, and at worst shared between everyone, only that we're all saying it in a different manner(and that I blured everything by going off tangent with my comment about Misato's possible hypocrisy )
summary:

(Shinji vs His Authorities)             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 27, 2013 12:40 pm [Split from Has Evangelion Changed Or Is It The Audience? - Monk] Chuckman wrote: This is what confuses me, as I try to figure it out. Shinji should, apparently , be listening to his peers and superiors, but what they're telling him is often, if not usually, wrong! What the hell is he supposed to do?
